content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95314
Technical Assistance/Administration . . . Executive Legal . . . Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660                           322-5901                    322-6441

March 17, 1976

Mr. Richard Boyle
c/o "Boyle for Senator Campaign"
554 Broadway
San Francisco, Ca. 94133

Re:  Request for Opinion
     No. 76-030

Dear Mr. Boyle:

     Thank you for your letter of March 1, 1976, requesting
an opinion with respect to the disclosure of a candidate's
political debts and related obligations.

     Government Code Section 83114 provides that "any person
may request the Commission to issue an opinion with respect
to his duties under the [Act]." Pursuant to this statutory
authorization, regulations adopted by the Commission require
that requests for opinions be submitted by the person whose
duties are in question or by that person's representative.
Your request poses a question concerning the duties of Robert
H. Mendelsonn, not your own duties. For these reasons, no
formal opinion will be issued. However, I hope that the
following informal comments will be helpful.

     Candidates and committees disclose their financial
conditions on campaign statements filed pursuant to appli-
cable provisions of the Political Reform Act. However, the
Act does not require disclosure of a debt existing at the
time when it took effect on January 7, 1975.

     If you have any questions, please contact Natalie West,
an attorney with the Legal Division of the Commission staff.

                                    Sincerely,

                                    [signature]
                                    Daniel H. Lowenstein
                                    Chairman,
                                    For the Commission

DHL:pvp

Government Code Section 83114 provides that "any person may request the Commission to issue an opinion with respect to his duties under the [Act]." Pursuant to this statutory authorization, regulations adopted by the Commission require that requests for opinions be submitted by the person whose duties are in question or by that person's representative. Your request poses a question concerning the duties of Robert H. Mendelsohn, not your own duties. For these reasons, no formal opinion will be issued. However, I hope that the following informal comments will be helpful. Candidates and committees disclose their financial conditions on campaign statements filed pursuant to applicable provisions of the Political Reform Act. However, the Act does not require disclosure of a debt existing at the time when it took effect on January 7, 1975. If you have any questions, please contact Natalie West, an attorney with the Legal Division of the Commission staff. Sincerely, Daniel H. Lowenstein Chairman, For the Commission The image you've provided is completely white, with no visible content or text.
